exemplar.title: To what extent did the surface area of a paper airplane (180.5,175.5,170.5,165.5,160.5) affect the horizontal displacement traveled in cm?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent did the surface area of a paper airplane (180.5,175.5,170.5,165.5,160.5) affect the horizontal displacement traveled in cm?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data recording and processing presented in logical, well-labelled tables with uncertainties and units
Correct calculation of averages, standard deviations and RÂ²=0.9958
Clear description of trend and processed data facilitates interpretation
weaknesses: Graphs lack axis labels, titles and error bars, reducing precision
No propagation of uncertainties or discussion of systematic errors
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Recording and processing of data are clear and mostly precise, warranting the top of the 3-4 band for communication. However, significant omissions in uncertainty propagation and graph labelling prevent full precision, aligning with 4/6 overall for data analysis.

strengths: Clear and specific context linking aerodynamic theory (lift, drag, Bernoulli) to the research question
Well-stated research question with precise variables and units
Detailed equipment list and step-by-step procedure that largely enable reproducibility
weaknesses: Minor language and notation errors in formulas (exponent typo in drag equation)
Ambiguities in methodological details such as rubber-band stretch length and launch angle
Lack of quantitative tolerances for controlled variables
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student embeds the research question in a focused aerodynamics context and provides background theory, meeting the 5-6 band descriptor for context. The procedural description and controlled-variable rationale allow reproduction with few ambiguities, placing methodological strands at the top of the 3-4 band. Overall performance merits a score of 5, reflecting strong contextualisation and mostly reproducible design with minor omissions.

strengths: Conclusion correctly restates the linear relationship observed between surface area and displacement
Consistent linkage to processed results without contradiction
weaknesses: Lacks quantitative citation of slope or numerical comparison to data
Literature comparison is superficial and qualitative without benchmarks
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is consistent with the analysis and meets the 3-4 band descriptor for relevance and support, but absence of quantitative justification precludes a higher mark. The scientific context comparison is limited, fitting the 1-2 band. Overall, a score of 3 reflects described but not fully justified conclusions.

strengths: Identification of specific methodological weaknesses (landing-point uncertainty, air currents, rubber-band fatigue)
Realistic and relevant improvement suggestions (video analysis, controlled environment, fresh bands) with brief justifications
Creative extension ideas to investigate temperature and material effects
weaknesses: Explanations of how improvements quantitatively reduce error are missing
Depth of discussion remains qualitative rather than comparing error magnitudes
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation describes specific methodological limitations and offers relevant improvements, meeting the 3-4 band for both strands. The student explains the impact of weaknesses and provides realistic remedies, justifying a score of 4 for evaluation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student states the research question clearly (âTo what extent did the surface area of a paper airplane â¦ affect the horizontal displacement travelled?â) and embeds it in a **specific context of aerodynamics, engineering relevance and personal motivation**.  Background theory explicitly links lift, drag and Bernoulliâs principle to the chosen variable, demonstrating why wing area should influence horizontal distance.  Because the context is tightly focused on paper-airplane flight rather than a generic âeffect of area,â it meets the descriptor of a **specific and appropriate context**.  Minor language errors do not obscure meaning, so a mark near the top of the 5-6 band is justified.

Uncertainties for surface area (Â±1 cmÂ²) and displacement (Â±3 cm) are quoted and a standard deviation is calculated, showing **some consideration of random error**.  However, **no propagation, no uncertainty bars on graphs and no discussion of instrumental/systematic errors** are provided.  The omissions are significant, so only limited evidence exists, corresponding to the 1-2 band; the presence of standard deviation justifies the upper mark within that band.

Six controlled variables are listed in two tables, each accompanied by a **rationale for control** that links back to lift, drag or displacement.  The student also justifies five surface-area levels and five repeats, showing awareness of the need for **relevant and sufficient data**.  Explanations are present but brief; for example, mass control is discussed, yet no quantitative tolerance is offered.  These points fulfil the descriptor of âdescribedâ methodological considerations, warranting the top of the 3-4 band.

Specific weaknesses are identified: Â±3 cm landing-point uncertainty, ambient air currents and rubber-band fatigue.  The student **discusses how each could alter horizontal displacement**, showing understanding of impact, but the depth is qualitative and not compared quantitatively against overall error.  This fits âdescribes specific weaknessesâ and partially explains their effect, meriting the upper end of the 3-4 band.

The equipment list, dimensions (with uncertainties) and a step-by-step procedure are provided, enabling another student to run a similar investigation.  **Key details such as catapult height, launch device, trial numbers and measurement tools are present.**  However, several ambiguities remain: the exact stretch length of the rubber band, the angle of launch, and how the wing-fold equation is applied in practice are not fully spelled out.  Consequently, reproduction is possible but would require some assumptions, placing the work solidly in the 3-4 band; the clearer portions merit the upper end of that band.

Averages, standard deviations and an RÂ² value (0.9958) are calculated correctly, and the trend is described.  **No further processing such as gradient, model equation or uncertainty analysis** is shown, and sig-fig consistency is weak.  Thus processing is broadly accurate but has notable omissions, aligning with the middle of the 3-4 band.

Improvements such as using a sand pit, video analysis, controlled indoor space and fresh rubber bands are **realistic and relevant**.  Each suggestion is accompanied by a short justification, but the explanations stop at a qualitative level (no indication of how much error would be reduced).  Hence the work fits securely in the 3-4 band, but not at its top because depth of explanation is modest.

Raw and processed data tables are included, units are shown, and uncertainties are quoted.  **Data are arranged logically and are easy to follow.**  Nevertheless, both bar charts are missing axis labels/titles, and one figure is duplicated with a placeholder name.  These lapses reduce precision, so the work is âclearâ but not consistently âpreciseâ, placing it at the top of the 3-4 band.

A short literature comparison mentions that drag decreases as surface area increases and cites section-2 theory, but **no numerical or detailed theoretical benchmark is used**.  The comparison is superficial and limited to qualitative agreement, matching the 1-2 band; inclusion of a couple of sources justifies the higher mark in that range.

The conclusion restates the observed linear increase in displacement with surface area and references the data (âaverage increase â¦ each increase in surface areaâ).  This is **largely consistent with the processed results**, but no quantitative comparison or slope is cited.  Therefore it is âdescribedâ rather than fully justified, meeting the 3-4 band but not the top of it.

annotations.comment: The introduction compellingly links personal motivation with aerodynamic theory, establishing relevance and engagement for the investigation.
Consider trimming the narrative to focus more tightly on scientific context and reducing anecdotal detail to improve concision.
The research question is stated clearly with specific variables and units, situating it firmly in the context of aerodynamics and paper-airplane design.
Formatting of the parenthesized values in the question is confusing; separate numerical levels from the interrogative to enhance readability.
Theoretical explanations of lift and drag are present but lack clear citations; include precise references for each principle to strengthen academic rigor.
Equation 1 is correctly presented but contains a typo in the exponent notation; revise to $$F_d=\tfrac12\rho v^2 C_d A$$ for clarity.
Equation 2 needs context: define constant c and confirm units so readers understand how lift scales with surface area.
The methodology section currently mentions âethnographic research designââthis is misplaced; focus on experimental design terminology.
Independent variable description lists levels and uncertainty; include a rationale for choosing exactly five levels to illustrate sufficiency.
Controlled-variable table is thorough; explanations link back to forces but quantitative tolerances (e.g. rubber-band stretch length) are omitted.
Consider specifying the exact angle of launch in degrees and method of alignment to ensure reproducibility of the catapult setup.
Raw data table includes uncertainties but raw values should use consistent significant figures to improve clarity.
Processed data table is clear and units are specified, facilitating interpretation of averages and standard deviations.
Graphs lack error bars and axis labels; add uncertainty bars and complete labels to demonstrate consideration of random and systematic errors.
No uncertainty propagation is shown in processed results; include calculations or discussion of how measurement errors affect the fitted line.
Lineâofâbestâfit description correctly references RÂ²=0.9958, supporting a linear model.
Discussion claims âabsence of notable errorsâ without quantification; critique residuals or outliers to substantiate model accuracy.
Conclusion restates the trend but lacks quantitative linkage to the slope; include numerical comparison of slope or percentage change.
Comparison to literature is qualitative and missing direct benchmarks; integrate specific data or studies to justify consistency with established findings.
Evaluation identifies specific weaknesses such as impact-point uncertainty and air currents, showing awareness of sources of error.
Improvements are realistic but remain qualitative; quantify the expected reduction in error if using video analysis or fresh rubber bands.
Extension ideas are creative and relevant, proposing new questions on temperature and material effects to broaden the investigation.

rubricData.name: Physics IA
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
